inline_comments.message:
Any specific reason to have an old springboot version?
As it's a new code, why not the latest one ? 2.5.0 ?

I don't see the commons-io used ?
and the latest version is 2.9.0
unneeded var, can be injected directly line 74

Changing to 2.5.0, also Ryan Sheehan is resolving maven versions across CLAMP and policy-arent (The PF parent pom), so we should switch the CLAMP runtime from 2.4.4 up to 2.5.0 as part of that work while we're at it.
We will bring in https later, we wanted to get something up for people to try out. Sub task https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-3334 covers https on the K8S participant.

What's the purpose of this file ? 
It's not used anywhere ?
It's an example TOSCA control lop file that has a kubernetes control loop element. It's to give people a guide on how to use the participant.
Could we move that to src/test/resources instead ? 😊
several of these don't appear to be referenced
this should not be specified in the dependency lists - the correct dependency version is specified in the parent pom
just verifying that this will be made configurable in a future review - may create the JIRA now or add a "TODO" comment
could do this in a single line

"//"?  - should be a checkstyle issue

same comment on other lines and in other files
what happens if getChart() doesn't find it?

any way to avoid the static data, as it makes the code less flexible and requires clear() or reset() methods to be invoked in any junits that use this class, whether directly or indirectly?  I frequently just create the data once and then pass it to all of the constructors that need it - not sure what would be the equivalent in Spring
should the key just be a UUID, if that's what is always used during look-up?
sonar: avoid containsKey/get combination - get() is sufficient by itself
maybe use a plain Map here since this just does a cast and can't actually force it into a LinkedTreeMap type
leave off "{}" and change e.getMessage() to just "e"
can just return repository here and then eliminate the check on line 96
if the process were to generate a lot of output, this could potentially block forever - need to read stdout & stderr before waiting for exit
maybe add a space after the ":"
can combine these lines using List.of()
could use .command() and avoid the need to invoke Arrays.asList()
can combine these lines (could even combine with line 190, too)
could use String.join() and shrink this to a one-liner (and avoid the need to call trim())
should classes like this be validated?  if so, should there be any validation annotations?

double logging?

same comment below (and elsewhere?)

add @Valid?

same question on the next line
should this be done in a separate init() method?
remove second "{}" and change to ", e"
maybe use the FileVisitor class/model instead
can does this in a single line by reading directly from the file, unless you want to see the json for debugging purposes
never mind - it throws an exception.  Should maybe return NOT_FOUND in that case?
prefer "@formatter:off" to disable automatic formatting.  (I assume that's the purpose of the trailing "//" on these lines.)
I update POLICY-3344 to say that http/https should be configurable.
I agree, and here I think putting // @formatter comments just clutters it more so I removed all format override and went with the default.
Changed the code to return NOT_FOUND, I think that is better.
Don't know why my eclipse isn't flagging these.
We just log this situation at the moment, when a control loop element gets into a confused state, we don't perform the state change, then the CLAMP supervision kicks in and reports the error.
I don't think {} works on exceptions.
oh, duh, was still "seeing" a logging statement here :-D
doesn't checkstyle prefer long variable names?

what I meant with my cryptic comment was to replace the text with the actual exception, so the stack trace isn't lost

same comment on line 134

This variable should not be static because each control loop element can have multiple charts and there can be more than one control loop element in each participant, particularly the K8S one.

It is changed to not be static.

Yes, this class should be restructured to spawn a thread in which the command is run, and the standard output/error should be monitored, otherwise it could hang.

Maybe also have a configured timeout value for the command.
My checkstyle is turned on in Eclipse but it doesn't flag this. Maybe it's OK to use short variable names for local variables. Anyway, I refactored this and renamed it "process"
I will raise another review to add supervision of the command execution.
I think we should decide how Spring validation and policy-common validation should work together.
@Jim, does the @NotNull/@NotBlank at class level will apply to these two fields?
I think this is called automatically in the Spring startup so I think all initiation has to be in the constructor.
Done, didn't know about this, it's nice.
I didn't like the use of "Database" in this class as this is not a database, it is using a local file system to cache/store charts.
I assume this will be done when you add supervision of the command execution

Using policy annotations, yes.  (Using hibernate annotations, NotNull works at the class level, but NotBlank only works at the field level - even though it allows you to specify it at the class level - go figure)
I think Spring supports some sort of init/destroy concept, too
this is fine, but could put this line after line 198 and then combine line 196 with 197
It is done on line 116 of this file in Patch Set 9
Should it follow usual conventions for URL paths as in api/pap .. with /policy/.. ?  If it should, could be added to the ticket that was opened by Saul.
I added this into Saul's JIRA https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-3368
planning to leave this as is?  or use Spring's version of an init method?
I'll take this in the next review that's coming up to handle the output and termination of commands correctly.
